items.summary: Replace the existing multi-crypto dashboard with a new full-page dashboard that displays the top 150 cryptocurrencies ranked by market cap. Fetch live data from the CoinGecko public API (markets endpoint, vs_currency=usd, per_page=150, page=1). Display each coin in a sortable, paginated table with columns: Rank, Name/Symbol, Price, 24h Change, Market Cap, 24h Volume, Buy Ratio (approximated as 24h volume / market cap × 100, shown as a percentage), and Fully Diluted Market Cap.

Add a filter/control bar above the top-150 table with dropdown selectors that allow the user to filter or sort the list by each of the following metrics individually: Live Market Cap (high to low / low to high), 24h Volume (high to low / low to high), Buy Ratio (high to low / low to high), and Fully Diluted Market Cap (high to low / low to high). Include a search input to filter coins by name or symbol. Include a reset button to clear all active filters.

Add a dedicated 'Price Projections' section below the main table. This section contains a coin selector dropdown (searchable, showing all 150 coins) and, once a coin is selected, displays a 12-month rolling price projection panel. The projection must show: a monthly price target table (Month 1 through Month 12 from today), a projected price range (low / mid / high) per month, a confidence score per month, and a line chart visualising the projected price trajectory over the 12-month period. Projections are algorithmically derived on the frontend using the coin's current price, 30-day historical trend from CoinGecko, and simple momentum/regression logic — no external AI API call required.

Implement a CoinGecko data-fetching layer using React Query with in-memory caching and rate-limit-safe request batching. Use the existing useQueries.ts pattern. Add a new hook (e.g. useTopCoins) that fetches the top 150 coins market data and a hook (e.g. useCoinHistory) that fetches 30-day historical price data for a selected coin. Both hooks must handle loading, error, and empty states gracefully and fall back to cached data on API errors.

Apply a cohesive dark-mode-first visual theme to the entire application. Use a dark charcoal/near-black base with neon cyan and gold accent colors. Use a clean sans-serif typeface. The header should display 'Crypto Tracker AI' branding. Cards, tables, and dropdowns should use subtle glassmorphism or frosted-panel styling. Positive price changes must render in emerald green, negative in red. The layout must be fully responsive down to 375 px mobile width.
